name: MP Materials Corp. / DE
business: MP Materials is the largest producer of rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere, owning and operating the Mountain Pass rare earth mine and processing facility in California and the Independence Facility in Texas for metal, alloy, and magnet manufacturing. The company produces rare earth oxides, metal, magnetic precursor products, and NdFeB permanent magnets, selling primarily to customers in Japan, South Korea, broader Asia, and the United States, and has ceased sales to China as of July 2025.

exposures.sentence: We own and operate the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing Facility (Mountain Pass) located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California, the only rare earth mining and processing site of scale in North America.

Certain rare earth elements (REE) serve as critical inputs for the rare earth magnets inside the electric motors, generators, and other components essential to automotive technologies, including those used in hybrid and electric vehicles (referred to collectively as xEVs).

Demand for rare earth permanent magnets is increasingly driven by applications beyond automotive, energy, and electronics, particularly service and humanoid robots and other physical AI applications, as well as critical defense systems.
Rare earth materials are used in a diverse array of end markets, including: ... Renewable Power Generation: wind power generators, for on- and offshore applications.
Critical Defense Systems: guidance and control systems, communications, avionics, global positioning systems, radar and sonar, drones, thermal barrier coatings and firearms.
Energy-Efficient Motors, Pumps and Compressors: heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) systems, elevators, escalators, consumer appliances and other industrial applications.

catalysts.event: Commenced manufacturing of NdFeB permanent magnets at Independence Facility
catalysts.sentence: At the end of 2025, the Company began commissioning the remaining commercial scale equipment for magnet manufacturing and commenced the manufacturing of its NdFeB permanent magnets.
The Company is currently advancing the construction of the HREE Facility, with commissioning expected in 2026.
The 10X Facility is expected to begin commissioning in 2028, and once completed and scaled, it will produce an estimated 7,000 MTs of magnets per year.

catalysts.event: Commenced sales of magnetic precursor products
catalysts.sentence: In the first quarter of 2025, we commenced sales of magnetic precursor products, primarily NdPr metal.

catalysts.event: Cessation of all product sales to China
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, to align with the terms of the DoW Transaction Agreements and in further support of our domestic supply chain objectives, we ceased all sales of our products to China.

tamClaims.claim: Aggregate global market for rare earth oxides totaled approximately 252,000 metric tons in 2025 and expected to grow at 6.0% CAGR through 2040
tamClaims.sentence: The aggregate global market for rare earth oxides (REO) totaled approximately 252,000 metric tons (MTs) in 2025 and is expected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 6.0% through 2040, according to research by Adamas Intelligence Inc. (Adamas).

tamClaims.claim: NdPr segment of REO market expected to grow at 8.4% CAGR through 2040
tamClaims.sentence: Further, Adamas estimates that the NdPr segment of the REO market, which makes up a significant majority of the market value, is expected to grow at an 8.4% CAGR through 2040, well in excess of the overall REO market.
